cards.prompt: Task.Run moves work to a pool thread: main != poolId and IsThreadPoolThread
cards.code: using System; using System.Threading; using System.Threading.Tasks; int main = Thread.CurrentThread.ManagedThreadId; int poolId = await Task.Run(()=> Thread.CurrentThread.ManagedThreadId); bool onPool = await Task.Run(()=> Thread.CurrentThread.IsThreadPoolThread); Console.WriteLine($"{main != poolId} {onPool}");

cards.prompt: CPU-bound Compute via Task.Run runs on a background thread (main != t)
cards.code: using System; using System.Threading; using System.Threading.Tasks; long Compute(){ long s=0; for(long i=0;i<50_000_000;i++) s+=i; return s; } int main=Thread.CurrentThread.ManagedThreadId; int t = await Task.Run(()=>{ Compute(); return Thread.CurrentThread.ManagedThreadId; }); Console.WriteLine(main != t);

cards.prompt: Task.Run does NOT speed up I/O: wrapped ~= direct (same)
cards.code: using System; using System.Diagnostics; using System.Threading.Tasks; async Task<int> Io(){ await Task.Delay(100); return 1; } var sw=Stopwatch.StartNew(); await Io(); long direct=sw.ElapsedMilliseconds; sw.Restart(); await Task.Run(async()=> await Io()); long wrapped=sw.ElapsedMilliseconds; Console.Write(Math.Abs(direct-wrapped) < 40 ? "same" : "diff");
